# Quick Launch — Feature Spec

## Overview

When the overlay opens with an **empty query** (idle state), show a curated "Quick Launch" row of frequently-used and pinned apps instead of a blank screen. Once the user types, Quick Launch fades out and normal search takes over.

**Model:** Hybrid — auto-generated from usage data + manual pin overrides.

---

## UX/UI Design

### Idle State (empty query)

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ 🔍  |                                              │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                     │
│  [icon]  Spotify                          ▶        │
│  [icon]  Visual Studio Code               ▶        │
│  [icon]  Discord                          ▶        │
│  [icon]  Chrome                           ▶        │
│  [icon]  Terminal                         ▶        │
│  [icon]  File Explorer                    ▶        │
│                                                     │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│       ↑ ↓ navigate   ↵ open   esc close            │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### Row Layout

```
[icon] [title                                    ] [launch-icon]
```

- **No subtitle** — keep it minimal, apps only
- **No kind label** — everything is an app, no need to show "App"
- **Right-side icon:** A small "arrow-right" (→) or "play" (▶) icon that appears on hover/selection, indicating "open this"
- **Selected state:** Standard selection highlight + the arrow icon appears

### Behavior

| Trigger | Behavior |
|---|---|
| Overlay opens (empty query) | Show Quick Launch rows |
| User types | Quick Launch hides, normal search results appear |
| User clears query (back to empty) | Quick Launch reappears |
| User navigates up/down | Standard row selection |
| User presses Enter | Launch the selected app |
| User presses Escape | Close overlay (same as before) |

### Pin/Unpin Flow

1. **During search:** Hover a result row → pin icon (📌) appears on the right
2. **Click pin icon:** App is added to Quick Launch pinned list
3. **Pin icon changes to filled:** 📌 (filled) = already pinned
4. **Click filled pin:** Removes from Quick Launch pinned list
5. **Visual feedback:** Brief toast notification "Added to Quick Launch" / "Removed from Quick Launch"

### Auto-Fill Logic

- Track app launch count in SQLite (add `launch_count` and `last_launched_at` columns to the apps index)
- Quick Launch = `pinned apps (in config order)` + `top N by launch_count` (fill remaining slots)
- Max items: **6** (configurable)
- If fewer than 6 apps have launch data, fill with alphabetically-sorted installed apps

---

## Data Model Changes

### Config (`config.toml`)

```toml
[quick_launch]
enabled = true
max_items = 6
# Apps pinned by the user — always appear first, in this order
pinned = []
# Auto-fill remaining slots from usage
auto_fill = true
```

### SQLite Index

Add columns to the apps table:

```sql
ALTER TABLE apps ADD COLUMN launch_count INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0;
ALTER TABLE apps ADD COLUMN last_launched_at INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0;
-- Unix timestamp (seconds) of last launch
```

### Rust Types

```rust
// apps/core/src/config.rs additions
pub struct QuickLaunchConfig {
    pub enabled: bool,
    pub max_items: usize,
    pub pinned: Vec<String>,  // app titles or paths
    pub auto_fill: bool,
}

// apps/core/src/overlay/model.rs additions
pub struct QuickLaunchItem {
    pub title: String,
    pub path: String,        // launch command or exe path
    pub icon_path: String,
    pub is_pinned: bool,
}
```

---

## IPC Changes

### Rust → JS (state push)

Add to the state snapshot JSON:

```json
{
  "quick_launch": {
    "visible": true,
    "items": [
      { "title": "Spotify", "path": "...", "icon": "data:...", "pinned": true },
      ...
    ]
  }
}
```

When `quick_launch.visible = true` and `rows` is empty (idle), JS renders Quick Launch rows instead of hiding the list.

### JS → Rust (actions)

New IPC message types:

```json
{ "t": "pin", "v": { "title": "Spotify" } }
{ "t": "unpin", "v": { "title": "Spotify" } }
```

---

## Icon Design

All new icons must match the existing overlay style:

### 1. Pin Icon (📌)

**Style:** 18×18 SVG, monoline stroke, rounded caps (matches search icon style)

```svg
<!-- Unpinned (outline) -->
<svg width="18" height="18" viewBox="0 0 18 18" fill="none" stroke="var(--text-faint)" stroke-width="1.5" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round">
  <path d="M10.5 2.5L15.5 7.5L11 12L12 15.5L8.5 12L3 12.5L5.5 8L2.5 4.5L6 3L10.5 2.5Z"/>
</svg>

<!-- Pinned (filled) -->
<svg width="18" height="18" viewBox="0 0 18 18" fill="var(--accent)" stroke="var(--accent)" stroke-width="1.5" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round">
  <path d="M10.5 2.5L15.5 7.5L11 12L12 15.5L8.5 12L3 12.5L5.5 8L2.5 4.5L6 3L10.5 2.5Z"/>
</svg>
```

### 2. Launch/Arrow Icon (▶)

**Style:** Same 18×18, appears on hover/selection only

```svg
<svg width="18" height="18" viewBox="0 0 18 18" fill="none" stroke="var(--text-faint)" stroke-width="1.5" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round">
  <path d="M7 5L13 9L7 13Z"/>
</svg>
```

### 3. Quick Launch Header (optional)

If we add a subtle label above the Quick Launch rows:

```svg
<!-- Lightning bolt for "Quick Launch" -->
<svg width="16" height="16" viewBox="0 0 16 16" fill="none" stroke="var(--text-faint)" stroke-width="1.5" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round">
  <path d="M9 1L3 9H8L7 15L13 7H8L9 1Z"/>
</svg>
```

---

## CSS Changes

```css
/* Quick Launch rows */
.row.quick-launch {
  /* Same base as .row */
}

.row.quick-launch .pin-icon {
  flex: 0 0 auto;
  width: 18px;
  height: 18px;
  opacity: 0;
  transition: opacity 120ms ease;
}

.row.quick-launch:hover .pin-icon,
.row.quick-launch.selected .pin-icon {
  opacity: 1;
}

.row.quick-launch .launch-icon {
  flex: 0 0 auto;
  width: 18px;
  height: 18px;
  opacity: 0;
  transition: opacity 120ms ease;
}

.row.quick-launch:hover .launch-icon,
.row.quick-launch.selected .launch-icon {
  opacity: 1;
}

/* Pin added toast */
.toast {
  position: fixed;
  bottom: 48px;
  left: 50%;
  transform: translateX(-50%);
  background: var(--sel);
  border: 1px solid var(--border);
  border-radius: var(--row-radius);
  padding: 6px 14px;
  font-size: 12px;
  color: var(--text-dim);
  opacity: 0;
  transition: opacity 200ms ease;
  pointer-events: none;
}

.toast.visible {
  opacity: 1;
}
```

---

## Implementation Phases

### Phase 1: Config + Data (Rust only)
1. Add `QuickLaunchConfig` to `config.rs` with defaults
2. Add `launch_count` + `last_launched_at` columns to SQLite schema
3. Increment DB version + migration
4. Add `record_launch(app_path)` function to `index_store.rs`
5. Add `get_quick_launch_items(pinned, max)` query function

### Phase 2: Overlay Model + Row Building
1. Add `QuickLaunchItem` to `overlay/model.rs`
2. Add `build_quick_launch_rows()` to `runtime_overlay_rows.rs`
3. Wire into idle state: when query is empty, push Quick Launch rows
4. Add `pin`/`unpin` IPC message handling in `runtime.rs`
5. Persist pin changes to `config.toml`

### Phase 3: JS/CSS UI
1. Add Quick Launch row rendering in `app.js`
2. Add pin icon + launch icon hover behavior
3. Add CSS for Quick Launch rows, icons, toast
4. Handle `pin`/`unpin` IPC messages from JS
5. Wire pin icon click → post("pin", ...) / post("unpin", ...)

### Phase 4: Integration
1. Call `record_launch()` on app execution (in `action_executor.rs`)
2. Test: pin 3 apps, restart overlay, verify they appear
3. Test: auto-fill with usage data
4. Test: search → pin → clear query → verify pinned app appears
5. Polish animations and transitions

---

## Open Questions

1. **Pin by title or path?** — Title can change (e.g., "Visual Studio Code" → "VS Code"). Path is stable but ugly in config. Recommendation: store path in config, display title in UI.

2. **Max pinned items?** — 6 total slots, so max pinned is 6 (but realistically 3-4 pinned + 2-3 auto-fill). Could add `max_pinned` config key later.

3. **Usage decay?** — Should old launches lose weight over time? Simple: just use raw count. Advanced: exponential decay. Start simple, add later if needed.

4. **Quick Launch in command mode?** — No. Command mode always shows command results. Quick Launch is idle-state only.
